title: Notice of 2026 Half-Year Results
gist: Glencore has scheduled its 2026 Half-Year Production Report for 29 July 2026 and its 2026 Half-Year Results for 5 August 2026, both released at 7.00 am UK via the LSE Regulatory News Service.

insights.claim: Glencore plc will release its 2026 Half-Year Production Report on Wednesday, 29 July 2026 at 7.00 am UK via the London Stock Exchange Regulatory News Service.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: Diary date for updated volume/production data across Glencore's commodity book ahead of the earnings print.
insights.quote: Glencore plc will release its 2026 Half-Year Production Report on Wednesday, 29 July 2026.

insights.claim: Glencore's 2026 Half-Year Results will be released on Wednesday, 5 August 2026 at 7.00 am UK, followed by a live audio webcast at 8.00 am UK.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: Key catalyst date for the H1 earnings, dividend/buyback signals and any trading-division commentary.
insights.quote: Our 2026 Half-Year Results will be released on Wednesday, 5 August 2026.

insights.claim: Glencore is a producer and marketer of more than 60 commodities with over 140,000 employees and contractors and a footprint in over 30 countries.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: Frames the diversified, multi-commodity exposure that makes Glencore a proxy for broad natural-resource demand rather than a single-metal play.
insights.quote: a major producer and marketer of more than 60 commodities

insights.claim: Glencore's principal customers are industrial consumers in the automotive, steel, power generation, battery manufacturing and oil sectors, and it also provides financing and logistics to commodity producers and consumers.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: Revenue leverage is tied to industrial and battery/EV demand, so those end-markets are the read-through for Glencore's cyclical earnings.
insights.quote: such as those in the automotive, steel, power generation, battery manufacturing and oil sectors
